summary: Small lbarchive cleanup/bugfix PR. It corrected variadic argument initialization in two lbArchive loaders, renamed the archive relocation routine from the address-style lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate across symbols/header/call sites, removed the standalone lbArchive_LoadSections implementation, and made a minor symbol_name shadowing cleanup. PR body and human review text were absent; intent is inferred from the title and diff. decomp.dev reported a GALE01 match regression because lbArchive_LoadSections disappeared from the lbarchive unit.

key_files.role: Corrected va_start anchors in lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC, removed lbArchive_LoadSections, cleaned symbol_name shadowing, and renamed lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate.
Updated the exported prototype from lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate.
Updated ftData_80085CD8 and ftData_80085E50 call sites to use lbArchiveRelocate for relocated copied fighter archive data.
Renamed the symbol at .text:0x80017340, size 0x17C, from lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate.

what_changed: Changed va_start(args, filename) to va_start(args, symbols) in lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC. Since symbols is the last fixed parameter before ..., this fixes the variadic list base.
Renamed lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate in lbarchive.c, lbarchive.h, config/GALE01/symbols.txt, and two ftdata.c call sites.
Deleted the standalone lbArchive_LoadSections implementation from lbarchive.c; no PR body explains the deletion.
Changed lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal to assign to the existing outer symbol_name variable instead of redeclaring const char* symbol_name inside the loop.
Kept the ftdata relocation call arguments unchanged, including the intptr_t base offset computed from the copied x59C buffer and original x59C source.
smart_moves: The va_start fix is a high-value correctness change: using filename would not point at the first true variadic argument for these loaders, while symbols is the correct last named parameter.
The semantic rename lbArchiveRelocate documents the function's apparent purpose while preserving the exact address and size in symbols.txt.
Updating the symbol map, C definition, header prototype, and ftdata callers in one PR avoided a half-renamed public function.
The symbol_name shadowing cleanup reduces confusing duplicate declarations in a vararg helper without changing the lookup/reporting logic.
decomp_lessons: For C variadic decomp functions, va_start must use the last named parameter before the ellipsis; otherwise vararg pairs such as void**/const char* can be read from the wrong slot.
When promoting an address-style name to a semantic name, update config symbols, declarations, definitions, and every cross-system caller together.
Deleting a matched function from a translation unit can show up as a decomp.dev broken match even if the source cleanup was intentional; document the rationale when match percentage drops.
Small scoping changes, such as removing an inner variable redeclaration, are worth tracking because they can affect both readability and codegen/matching in decomp work.
Archive relocation call sites that copy data into a new buffer should preserve the base_addr pointer-difference pattern shown in ftdata unless the relocation API is reverified.
naming_conventions: Use module prefix plus behavior for known lbarchive routines: lbArchiveRelocate instead of lbArchive_80017340.
Retain numbered lbArchive_80017xxx names for functions whose behavior is not yet confidently named.
C symbol names and config/GALE01/symbols.txt names should match exactly when a function receives a real name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The rename at .text:0x80017340 retained the same address and size: this was a symbol/name cleanup, not a code movement.
The va_start anchor is a source-level detail with ABI/runtime consequences; verify generated vararg access after changing variadic function signatures.
Removing the inner const char* declaration in lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal is a codegen-sensitive cleanup knob: same logical variable, narrower diff than rewriting the loop.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code 55.87% (-0.01%, -240 bytes) with one broken match: main/melee/lb/lbarchive lbArchive_LoadSections went from 100.00% to 0.00%.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.note: GALE01 report flagged matched code decrease of -240 bytes and one broken match for lbArchive_LoadSections.
The slice contains no PR body, reviews, or review comments, so there is no human reviewer rationale to extract.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for lbArchive_LoadSections to see whether the decomp.dev broken match was restored or intentionally accepted.
Audit other lbArchive or lbdvd variadic wrappers for va_start using a parameter other than the last fixed argument.
Check all references to lbArchive_80017340 and lbArchiveRelocate to ensure no stale numbered-name call sites remain.
Compare lbArchiveRelocate behavior against HSD archive relocation structures to confirm the semantic name and base_addr handling.
